basics.name: Jaeho Cho
basics.label: Electrical Engineering Student
basics.summary: An ambitious Electrical Engineering student at The Cooper Union, specializing in bioengineering projects and passionate about developing innovative solutions to enhance human life.

work.name: The Cooper Union
work.summary: Working on an open-source robotic solution for real-time motion tracking and replication to custom humanoid arms. Contributed to designing a PCB for an eTextile.
highlights: Developed a robotic solution with 8 degrees of freedom.
Designed a PCB for an eTextile presented at the ACM student research competition.

work.name: The Cooper Union
work.position: Teacher Assistant in Design and Drawing
work.summary: Taught engineering design fundamentals and Onshape CAD to high school students. Led Arduino workshops culminating in students building 4-DOF robotic arms.
highlights: Curated lesson plans and led hands-on workshops.

education.institution: The Cooper Union for the Advancement of Science and Art

projects.name: Prosthetic Hand
projects.summary: Developed an original actuation system design to optimize dexterity and designed a 3D-printed myoelectric prosthetic hand.

projects.name: Assistive Devices: Robotic Glove
projects.summary: Researched, designed, and built an electronic glove to assist individuals with limited hand mobility.
highlights: Designed to assist with hand movement for people with limited mobility.
Presented prototype and research at the EID101 2022 Final Showcase.

projects.name: Game Development: Boxing Game
projects.summary: Developing an original boxing game utilizing the Unity game engine and C# scripts, showcasing advanced gameplay mechanics.
highlights: Utilizes Unity game engine for development.
Collaborative project with an NYU Computer Science undergraduate.
